title: Raw Pointers
content:
Raw pointers (*const T and *mut T) are Rust's equivalent of C pointers. They can be null, dangling, or unaligned. Creating raw pointers is safe -- only dereferencing them requires unsafe.

Raw pointers are needed for FFI (calling C code), implementing low-level data structures, and performance-critical code that needs to bypass borrow checking.

Critical rules: never dereference a null or dangling pointer, ensure proper alignment, and be aware of aliasing rules (two &mut pointers to the same memory is undefined behavior).

codeExamples.description: Safe abstraction over raw pointers: split_at_mut

keyPoints: Creating raw pointers is safe; dereferencing them is unsafe
*const T is immutable, *mut T is mutable (but this is not enforced by the compiler)
Raw pointers can be null, dangling, or misaligned
Use is_null() to check for null before dereferencing
ptr.add(n) and ptr.offset(n) do pointer arithmetic
Wrap raw pointer operations in safe abstractions whenever possible
